content:
Character: Brandon Calloway
Perception: Brandon is hard to miss — tall, handsome, that theatrical charisma turned up to eleven. He lights up every room, which is both his gift and his armor. He's a good actor — I've heard him run lines at the coffee shop and there's real emotional depth there, more than his easygoing persona suggests. He's kind in a way that feels instinctive, not performative. We've been talking more lately; he's becoming a friend I didn't expect to make. I think he sees me as someone interesting he hasn't figured out yet. I know he's struggling with his career more than he shows, that he keeps the building together socially, and that his confidence took a serious hit recently — something about an audition he won't fully discuss.

He's kind in a way that feels instinctive, not performative. We've been talking more lately; he's becoming a friend I didn't expect to make. I think he sees me as someone interesting he hasn't figured out yet. I know he's struggling with his career more than he shows, that he keeps the building together socially, and that his confidence took a serious hit recently — something about an audition he won't fully discuss.
